html:
A Mandrake is a passive Mob found in Mandrake Forests. They are very rare and only 2-5 Mandrakes will spawn per World. They usually spawn in the same area and always on Grass Turf. No Mobs will eat Mandrakes that are still planted in the ground.

Mandrakes cannot be grown and there is no way to get more live Mandrakes. The player can only get Mandrake as an item from the Loot Stash, which spawns every Winter.

With normal damage modifiers, it takes one hit from a Spear to kill a Mandrake (any version of an Axe or Pickaxe will do the same). All forms of the Mandrakes are completely immune to fire and cannot be set on fire.

Only if a planted Mandrake is burned, a new one can regrow after 12.5 days. This regrowth time is not affected by seasons, weather, or time of the day. It will attempt to respawn within 5 tiles of the original location, on the same terrain, avoiding roads, structures and other things that could block it and there must be no players nearby.

Behavior

When picked at Dusk or Night, it will turn into a living Mandrake, following the player around, even through Wormholes, and constantly making noise until morning, when it will replant itself on the spot. Like Pig, it will back away when the player gets too close. The player can chase and kill it, and the mandrake will die and become an item.

When picked during the Day, it will die instantly and can be collected as an item.

Mandrake

Raw Mandrakes heal 60 Health and 75 Hunger and do not spoil. When eaten, Mandrakes will put all players and Mobs within 3.75 tiles radius to sleep for 10 seconds.

Werepigs, Gobblers, Bearger, and Pengulls will eat Raw Mandrakes left on the ground, putting themselves and all nearby Mobs and players to sleep.

Mandrakes can be used to make the Pan Flute and can also be used to make Mandrake Soup in a Crock Pot. Eating Mandrake Soup restores 100 Health, 150 Hunger, and 5 Sanity, but does not put anyone to sleep.

Cooked Mandrake

Cooking a Mandrake will put all player and Mobs within 6.25 tiles radius to sleep for 10 seconds. Eating a Cooked Mandrake restores 100 Health and 150 Hunger, as well as putting all player and Mobs to sleep like cooking it.

Pigs, Bunnymen, Gobblers, Bearger, and Pengulls will eat Cooked Mandrakes left on the ground, putting themselves and all nearby Mobs and players to sleep.

Cooked Mandrake cannot be put into Crock Pot and have no other uses than eating.

Usage

Will put the player to sleep.

×5

Will put all nearby Mobs to sleep.

Tips

  • Since Mandrakes are extremely fast creatures and will keep their distance from the player even if they follow them, it's recommended to only pick them during the day if this is the item the player wants. If one is picked at dusk while the player has no weapons, it is best to let it replant itself in the morning because punching the Mandrake will cause it to run away from the player, where it might be killed and eaten by something else.
  • When picking a planted Mandrake during Dusk or Night, the player can try to force attack it by clicking on the Mandrake with the mouse while holding the force attack key.
  • Live Mandrakes can be attacked and eaten by Werepigs, which will still trigger the sleep effects.
  • A riled-up Abigail will not attack a live mandrake.
  • One should usually eat cooked mandrakes as they are instead of making mandrake soup since it does not spoil and the sleep effect can be beneficial.
    • Despite this, the player is not advised to do so, as it is not easy to get more Mandrakes.

Trivia

  • On post "Relese Notes 66529" there was description "There are also reports of a mysterious new creature hiding somewhere on the island. We found the following sound recording in the remnants of Wilson’s camp:" after that there was a Gramaphon with text "Wilson's field recording 1069". When user clicks on Gramaphon, it starts making Mandrake sounds. [1]
  • The Mandrake (raw and cooked) is one of the few foods in the game that do not Spoil, along with raw/hatching Tallbird Eggs, Guardian's Horn, and Deerclops Eyeball. However, Mandrake Soup will still spoil.
  • Ghosts will often float above Mandrakes.
  • There is a page on the Klei website that is nothing more than multiple looping gifs of hopping Mandrakes along with looping audio of the "meep" sound they make.
  • The Mandrake, Mandragora officinarum as per Wickerbottom's identification, is a real-life plant that has roots in European folklore. Its roots can bear a vaguely human shape and were said to scream when uprooted. The sleep effect caused by cooking and eating Mandrakes is based on the myth that those who hear the scream of a mandrake die, which itself is based around the fact that the real plant can cause unconsciousness when eaten in sufficient quantities.

Gallery

  • Original HD Mandrake icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Original HD Cooked Mandrake icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
  • Dead, planted, and recently deceased Mandrakes.
  • Two hopping Mandrakes.
  • A Mandrake found underground.
  • Three mandrakes following Wilson.
  • A Mandrake mid-hop.
  • Hounds asleep due to eating Mandrakes.
  • A drawing of Wendy and Webber taking pictures of a live Mandrake from Rhymes With Play.
  • A Mandrake plush.
  • The Mandrake is a mystery accessory for the Blind Box Figures.

“I've heard strange things about those plants.”
–Wilson, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“That's not a normal plant.”
–Willow, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“Is strange plant.”
–Wolfgang, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“The mighty mandrake. I should pick it at night.”
–Wendy, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“IT IS A PLANT”
–WX-78, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“Mandragora officinarum.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“It's looking at me.”
–Woodie, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“What a disturbing root.”
–Maxwell, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“The appearance of that root raises my curiosity.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“A rutabaga!”
–Wigfrid, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“That's a funny looking plant.”
–Webber, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“A mystical plant.”
–Walani, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“Have I discovered a new root vegetable?!”
–Warly, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“A plant o'legend.”
–Woodlegs, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“'TIS CARROT?”
–Wilba, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“Hiding”
–Wormwood, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“Swell. I picked up an annoying sidekick.”
–Wheeler, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“That's a weird shrub.”
–Winona, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“It's a prank plant.”
–Wortox, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“Supposed to pick those at night, florp.”
–Wurt, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“Hey, what's that hiding down there?”
–Walter, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“What is that, a turnip?”
–Wanda, when examining a planted Mandrake.
“Cut it out!”
–Wilson, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“Now that's just creepy!”
–Willow, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“Little plant man friend!”
–Wolfgang, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“Onward, my vegetable minion!”
–Wendy, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“WHAT DO YOU WANT?”
–WX-78, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“Mandragora officinarum. But with a face.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“What have I done?”
–Woodie, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“Oh shut UP, will you?”
–Maxwell, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“Its screech is of a remarkably annoying register.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“She just wants tö gö ön a rutabaga saga.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“You're a bad friend!”
–Webber, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“You're messing up my chill vibes, dude.”
–Walani, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“Do not pick! Do not pick!”
–Warly, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“Quit yer squawkin'!”
–Woodlegs, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“IT DOTH PROTEST TOO MUCH, METHINKS”
–Wilba, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“Merp! Merp! Meep! Merp!”
–Wormwood, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“Give me one good reason why I shouldn't just launch you out of the Pew-matic.”
–Wheeler, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“This is exactly what having a little sister's like.”
–Winona, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“Stop that or I'll steal your voice.”
–Wortox, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“Glargh! It following me!”
–Wurt, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“Follow the leader!”
–Walter, when examining a picked Mandrake.
“You can stop chiming in any time!”
–Wanda, when examining a picked Mandrake.

“Mandrake roots have strange properties.”
–Wilson, when examining a dead Mandrake.
“Why did this plant have a face?”
–Willow, when examining a dead Mandrake.
“Little plant man is died!”
–Wolfgang, when examining a dead Mandrake.
“Alas, poor mandrake!”
–Wendy, when examining a dead Mandrake.
“I HAVE KILLED IT. GOOD.”
–WX-78, when examining a dead Mandrake.
“Mandragora officinarum. Deceased.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining a dead Mandrake.
“Sorry, little guy.”
–Woodie, when examining a dead Mandrake.
“My ears are still ringing.”
–Maxwell, when examining a dead Mandrake.
“It appears to be adverse to light rays.”
–Wagstaff, when examining a dead Mandrake.
“The cörpse öf the rutabaga still retains its magic.”
–Wigfrid, when examining a dead Mandrake.
“The skin is all seared.”
–Webber, when examining a dead Mandrake.
“I was rooting for it.”
–Walani, when examining a dead Mandrake.
“I should like to get to the root of this mystery...”
–Warly, when examining a dead Mandrake.
“Twas a noisy little beast.”
–Woodlegs, when examining a dead Mandrake.
“'TIS DEAD”
–Wilba, when examining a dead Mandrake.
“Need friend?”
–Wormwood, when examining a dead Mandrake.
“Forgive me if I don't mourn your death.”
–Wheeler, when examining a dead Mandrake.
“Dead as a doornail.”
–Winona, when examining a dead Mandrake.
“It's out of prank juice.”
–Wortox, when examining a dead Mandrake.
“It not moving anymore.”
–Wurt, when examining a dead Mandrake.
“It's got a little face!”
–Walter, when examining a dead Mandrake.
“Oh, I can't bear to look at it...”
–Wanda, when examining a dead Mandrake.

“Poor little guy.”
–Wilson
“The fire didn't get rid of its face. Gives me the willies.”
–Willow
“Little plant man is food?”
–Wolfgang
“It was a good death. You will be delicious.”
–Wendy
“THAT'S WHAT YOU GET FOR BEING PATHETIC”
–WX-78
“Mandragora officinarum, cooked in the name of discovery.”
–Wickerbottom
“Was that a bad thing to do?”
–Woodie
“It has lost a lot of its power.”
–Maxwell
“She's definitely dead.”
–Wigfrid
“Cooked to death.”
–Webber
“Could use horseradish...”
–Warly
“Smells sleepy”
–Wormwood
“Dead as several doornails.”
–Winona
“It's at peace now.”
–Wortox
“Feel bit weird eating veggie with face, florp.”
–Wurt
“Sorry little buddy.”
–Walter
“It was a matter of survival.”
–Wanda

Mandrakes can be used to make the [[Pan Flute]] and can also be used to make [[Mandrake Soup]] in a [[Crock Pot]]. Eating [[Mandrake Soup]] restores 100 Health, 150 Hunger, and 5 Sanity, but does not put anyone to sleep. {{clear}} == Cooked Mandrake == {{Object Infobox |image = Cooked Mandrake Dropped.png |icon = Cooked Mandrake.png |name = Cooked Mandrake |hpRestored = 100 |hungerRestored = 150 |sanityRestored = 0 |spoil = Does not spoil |stack = 40 |foodType = {{foodType|Veggie}} |spawnCode = "cookedmandrake" }} {{Quotes |walter=Sorry little buddy. |wanda=It was a matter of survival. |warly=Could use horseradish... |wigfrid=She's definitely dead. |maxwell=It has lost a lot of its power. |webber=Cooked to death. |wendy=It was a good death. You will be delicious. |wickerbottom=Mandragora officinarum, cooked in the name of discovery. |willow=The fire didn't get rid of its face. Gives me the willies. |wilson=Poor little guy. |winona=Dead as several doornails. |wolfgang=Little plant man is food? |woodie=Was that a bad thing to do? |wormwood=Smells sleepy |wortox=It's at peace now. |wurt=Feel bit weird eating veggie with face, florp. |wx78=THAT'S WHAT YOU GET FOR BEING PATHETIC |choose=wx78 }} [[Cooking]] a Mandrake will put all player and Mobs within 6.25 tiles radius to sleep for 10 seconds. Eating a Cooked Mandrake restores 100 Health and 150 Hunger, as well as putting all player and Mobs to sleep like cooking it. [[Pig]]s, [[Bunnyman|Bunnymen]], [[Gobbler]]s, [[Bearger/DST|Bearger]], and [[Pengull]]s will eat Cooked Mandrakes left on the ground, putting themselves and all nearby Mobs and players to sleep. Cooked Mandrake cannot be put into Crock Pot and have no other uses than eating. {{clear}} == {{pic|32|Icon Tools}} Usage == {{Recipe |item = Mandrake |tool = Cooking |result = Cooked Mandrake |note = Will put the player to sleep. }} {{Recipe |item1 = Mandrake |item2 = Filler |item3 = Filler |item4 = Filler |tool = Crock Pot |result = Mandrake Soup }} {{Recipe |item3 = Cut Reeds |count3 = 5 |item2 = Rope |item1 = Mandrake |tool = Prestihatitator |result = Pan Flute }} {{Recipe |item = Cooked Mandrake |manure = yes |result = Manure |note = Will put all nearby Mobs to sleep. }} =={{Pic|32|Prototype}} Tips== * Since Mandrakes are extremely fast creatures and will keep their distance from the player even if they follow them, it's recommended to only pick them during the day if this is the item the player wants. If one is picked at dusk while the player has no weapons, it is best to let it replant itself in the morning because punching the Mandrake will cause it to run away from the player, where it might be killed and eaten by something else. * When picking a planted Mandrake during Dusk or Night, the player can try to force attack it by clicking on the Mandrake with the mouse while holding the force attack key. * Live Mandrakes can be attacked and eaten by [[Werepig]]s, which will still trigger the sleep effects. * A riled-up [[Abigail]] will not attack a live mandrake. * One should usually eat cooked mandrakes as they are instead of making mandrake soup since it does not spoil and the sleep effect can be beneficial. ** Despite this, the player is not advised to do so, as it is not easy to get more Mandrakes. == {{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * On post "Relese Notes 66529" there was description "There are also reports of a mysterious new creature hiding somewhere on the island. We found the following sound recording in the remnants of Wilson’s camp:" after that there was a Gramaphon with text "Wilson's field recording 1069". When user clicks on Gramaphon, it starts making Mandrake sounds. (Archive) [https://web.archive.org/web/20121114065654/http://www.dontstarvegame.com/blog/ Don't Starve blog] in September 2012 * The Mandrake (raw and cooked) is one of the few foods in the game that do not [[Spoil]], along with raw/hatching [[Tallbird Egg]]s, [[Guardian's Horn]], and [[Deerclops Eyeball]]. However, Mandrake Soup will still spoil. * [[Ghost]]s will often float above Mandrakes. * There is a [https://web.archive.org/web/20150315152837/http://www.dontstarvegame.com/science/mandrake/meep.html page] on the Klei website that is nothing more than multiple looping gifs of hopping Mandrakes along with looping audio of the "meep" sound they make. * The Mandrake, ''[[Wikipedia:Mandragora officinarum|Mandragora officinarum]]'' as per Wickerbottom's identification, is a real-life plant that has roots in European folklore. Its roots can bear a vaguely human shape and were said to scream when uprooted. The sleep effect caused by cooking and eating Mandrakes is based on the myth that those who hear the scream of a mandrake die, which itself is based around the fact that the real plant can cause unconsciousness when eaten in sufficient quantities. =={{Pic|32|Blueprint}} Gallery== CD mandrake.png|Original HD Mandrake icon from Bonus Materials from [[CD Don't Starve]].
